sk-design mode-registry is the routing/advisor source of truth, not a full command-surface schema
impeccable-main uses command-metadata.json as the single source for command descriptions and argument hints read by build and pin surfaces

focus: [D2-12/D2] command description as auto-trigger keyword surface - does the agent ever pick a specific /design:* command, or collapse to the hub? (corpus: impeccable-main)

graphEvents.label: Do /design command descriptions cause agents to select specific pinned commands, or only wake the sk-design hub?
Advisor replay and registry contracts route natural-language design prompts to the single sk-design skill, not to /design:* commands
Current /design:* descriptions help explicit command/menu use but are not a deterministic auto-trigger surface
impeccable-main uses broad parent trigger plus in-skill subcommand routing, with optional pinned shortcuts when standalone command triggering is desired
Command metadata must label each description's routing role: hub keyword, direct command help, pinned shortcut trigger, or generated docs

focus: [D2-13/D2] /design:* allowed-tools over-grant (all grant Write/Edit/Bash though 4 modes are read-and-guide) (corpus: impeccable-main)

mode-registry separates reference-base from playwright-extract backends but carries no command tool policy
impeccable-main uses metadata-first command descriptions and constrained script tool grants rather than blanket raw write/edit access

focus: [D3-A1/D3] missing hub-level deterministic router: project mode-registry.json into a parseable prompt->workflowMode router for replay

graphEvents.label: How can sk-design parent-to-mode routing be made deterministic and replayable from prompt to workflowMode?
The sk-design parent hub has prose routing rules but no parseable router model; existing router-replay returns parseable:false on the hub
mode-registry.json stores mode identity, packets, aliases and advisorRouting, but not default, weighting, ambiguity or multi-axis selection policy
Nested mode packets such as design-interface already expose DEFAULT_RESOURCE, INTENT_SIGNALS and RESOURCE_MAP for deterministic replay
Existing skill-benchmark router replay and D5 connectivity machinery can enforce a hub router once the hub exposes a parseable registry projection
The parent hub must expose a machine-readable prompt-to-workflowMode router, or routing cannot be replayed independently of prose

focus: [D3-A2/D3] self-attested vs content-bound proof of utilization: proof must echo a value that exists only in the loaded file or carry a content hash

graphEvents.label: What proves a selected sk-design packet and shared context were used rather than merely named as loaded?
The context-loaded and proof-of-application cards require loaded-file and evidence fields, but no file hash, anchor echo, or unique content value
proof_check.py verifies proof-field labels and READY, but not file paths, digests, anchor echoes, or line-bound content
The live skill-benchmark evidence records activation, stated resources, tool calls, and observed read paths, but not content-bound utilization proof
A sourceProof schema using sha256 or verified anchor echoes can make utilization proof deterministic on the local repo
A utilization proof must bind each claimed loaded file to current file content via sha256 or a checker-verified echoed anchor before READY or benchmark pass claims

focus: [D3-A3/D3] registry-as-source-of-truth is fictional at runtime; resolve never-hardcode vs hardcoded-default by making registry router policy the source and generated projections the runtime surfaces

graphEvents.label: What does registry-as-source-of-truth mean when advisor activation and hub mode selection do not read the registry at runtime?
mode-registry is identity and projection data, not a live runtime mode router
The interface default is hardcoded in hub prose while the registry has no routerPolicy default
Advisor can watch or score registry path metadata but does not parse registry aliases or mode policy for routing
Existing packet router replay gates parseability but parent hubs need a registry-router adapter
Registry source-of-truth is enforceable only when registry fields generate or drift-check every runtime projection that claims to route design modes

focus: [D3-A4/D3] ambiguous/multi-axis prompt resolution undefined; weighted hub scoring with ambiguity delta, tie-breaks, and ordered mode bundles are missing from sk-design routing

graphEvents.label: How should the sk-design hub resolve prompts where multiple design modes score near each other or represent separate axes?
The sk-design hub has prose for smallest useful mode and mode pairing, but no weights, ambiguity delta, tie-breaks, compatibility rules, or router return shape
Existing router-replay keeps near-tied intents and unions resources, but parent design routing needs primary mode, ordered roles, and defer reasons
Skill-benchmark modePrecision is intentionally advisory and single-mode, so wrong parent mode selection can stay outside weighted gating
Build work requires interface plus foundations and optional support modes, but the route result cannot currently express a required ordered bundle
A parent design hub router must deterministically return exactly one of single mode, ordered mode bundle, or deferReason, with traceable scores and tie-breaks

focus: [D3-A5/D3] negative routing: design taste prompts rank sk-design first and Figma/Open Design transports below it

graphEvents.label: Can advisor replay prove taste prompts rank sk-design first while Figma/Open Design transports stay below it?
The deterministic Python advisor probe ranks sk-design first, mcp-open-design third, and mcp-figma fifth for a taste-heavy design prompt
Open Design and Figma transport skills explicitly defer design judgment and taste to sk-design
Skill-benchmark D1-inter can score one expected skill rank, but cannot yet express a rank-below transport list
For taste-oriented design prompts, sk-design must rank above Figma/Open Design transport skills; transports may appear only below the taste authority

focus: [D3-A6/D3] registry completeness + alias-collision static audit for sk-design hub

graphEvents.label: Can the sk-design registry prove every mode is reachable, every alias has one owner, and local intent coverage is measurable?
The live registry has five mode rows, five existing packet SKILL.md files, matching packetSkillName values, and zero normalized alias collisions
The raw hub keyword projection has 47 of 101 non-infrastructure keywords not covered by registry aliases, a 46.5% uncovered projection rate
The current skill-benchmark D5/router replay path parses INTENT_SIGNALS and RESOURCE_MAP, not hub mode-registry aliases or packet reachability
Hub-mode skills need a deterministic registry audit before packet router replay: mode parity, packet reachability, packet-name parity, alias uniqueness, and explicit corpus coverage

focus: [D3-A7/D3] utilization invisible in live dispatch: require a machine-readable ROUTED workflowMode declaration

graphEvents.label: How can live sk-design dispatch prove the selected workflowMode rather than only proving stated resources?
The live benchmark prompt and parser capture surface/resources/assets, but no sk-design workflowMode or ordered mode bundle
parseLiveResult always returns observedIntents: [], so live expected intentKeys can score intentRecall=0 even when resources are present
The context manifest proves loaded design files, but it does not prove the route decision that made those files required
Existing modePrecision is explicitly advisory, so it cannot fail missing or wrong live mode declarations
Live parent-hub utilization must emit a registry-valid ROUTED workflowMode token before context manifest and proof rows can satisfy routing enforcement

focus: [D3-A8/D3] tool-permission correctness across the reference-base vs playwright-extract backendKind split

graphEvents.label: Can sk-design prove that a selected workflowMode uses the backendKind and tool surface declared by the registry?
mode-registry declares backendKind but does not carry allowedTools, forbiddenTools, mutatesWorkspace, or Bash allowlist fields
Four reference-base packets are read-and-guide while md-generator alone grants Write/Edit/Bash for the Playwright extraction pipeline
The live benchmark records toolCalls but does not score them against backendKind or forbidden-tool policy
reference-base may optionally use external design-reference lookup, but that is distinct from Playwright extraction or workspace mutation
A parent design route must bind workflowMode to registry backendKind and a checked toolSurface before resource recall or readiness proof can pass

focus: [D3-A9/D3] sub-agent/small-model dispatch utilization across the context boundary: out-of-band proof or pre-load-by-construction

graphEvents.label: How can sk-design prove delegated child or small-model utilization across a context boundary?
The design contract requires child dispatch context/proof cards, but current proof_check validates card shape rather than child prompt receipt or parent-verified boundary linkage
Generic Agent I/O evidence is optional/advisory and absent metadata never blocks, so it cannot serve as sk-design's hard boundary gate
cli-opencode already has a design/UI dispatch template that pre-loads sk-design files and proof cards by construction, but benchmark fixtures do not score boundary proof
cli-codex and cli-claude-code checked templates lack the explicit sk-design boundary proof fields present in cli-opencode's design/UI template
skill-benchmark first-failing stages and live evidence omit design boundary proof, child cards, and parent proof_check validation
Delegated design output may be adopted only when route declaration, required design context, child proof cards, and parent-side validation are tied by a boundary proof record

focus: [D3-A10/D3] standing routing self-test gold corpus for prompt->expected workflowMode, hint-free plus adversarial minimal pairs

graphEvents.label: Can sk-design routing be measured by a private-gold prompt-to-workflowMode corpus before utilization proof is scored?
sk-design has stable workflowMode labels, but the deterministic benchmark scores intent/resource primitives rather than a hard hub-route primitive
Lane C already has the right public/private and contamination-lint mechanism, but private expected gold lacks workflowMode and minimal-pair fields
Existing modePrecision is explicitly advisory and cannot enforce parent-hub workflowMode selection
sk-design route regressions are near-neighbor confusions, so the corpus needs adversarial minimal pairs rather than only one happy-path fixture per mode
For parent-mode skills, expected workflowMode or routeOutcome must pass before resource recall, context manifests, or proof cards can count as successful utilization

focus: [D3-A11/D3] default-to-interface as a silent miss sink -- separate routed from routed-correctly; measure false-default rate; ambiguous->ask vs silent default

graphEvents.label: How should sk-design distinguish routed from routed-correctly when generic prompts silently default to interface?
The sk-design hub defaults generic design prompts to interface, but has no typed routeOutcome, defaultApplied, askRequired, or deferReason contract
mode-registry has enough alias corpus to measure false defaults, but stores no ambiguity/default policy fields
The skill-benchmark funnel gates intent/resource routing while modePrecision remains advisory, so wrong workflowMode or missing ask/defer is not a hard failure
The live parser exposes statedRoutingCorrect but currently leaves it null and does not parse workflowMode/defaultApplied/routeOutcome
A local alias proxy found a strict false-default rate of 29/46 (0.63) and a generous false-default rate of 4/46 (0.087), proving the metric is sensitive to untyped prose policy
Resource recall, context manifests, proof cards, and READY claims may count only after a parent-hub route is both present and correct against private gold
When private gold expects defer/ask for ambiguity, a silent interface default is a false default even if interface resources load

focus: [D3-A12/D3] four-copy intent-vocabulary drift across graph metadata, hub keywords/prose, mode-registry aliases, and packet INTENT_SIGNALS; define a classified projection drift gate

graphEvents.label: How can sk-design make intent vocabulary drift between graph metadata, hub prose, registry aliases, and packet INTENT_SIGNALS loud and blocking?
sk-design has four real intent-vocabulary copies with no machine-declared projection ownership
The current skill-benchmark router and D5 gates parse packet INTENT_SIGNALS/RESOURCE_MAP, not graph metadata or mode-registry alias drift
The existing sk-code router-sync test is the right local precedent: pin the checkable projections, not every prose nuance
Vocabulary drift and near-tie/default policy fit a sibling hub-router.json better than overloading mode-registry identity rows
Parent-hub route corpus and utilization proof may score only after classified vocabulary projections pass

focus: [D3-A13/D3] loaded vs applied: an output-diff witness that names a concrete output choice traceable to a mode rule and classifies proof as not-loaded, loaded-inert, or loaded-determinative

graphEvents.label: How can sk-design prove loaded mode context changed a concrete output choice rather than only proving files were loaded?
Proof checker validates proof-field presence and READY, not a concrete output choice caused by a loaded mode rule
Live mode and D4-R measure routing or task-output quality, but expose no applicationWitnesses tying output choices to source rules
Existing sk-design playbooks contain choice-level expected signals, but the scenario loader preserves only resources/assets/pass criteria as scorer gold
READY, adoption, or task-outcome credit may count only after at least one required loaded-determinative application witness is present
Loaded-vs-applied proof must classify each required witness as not-loaded, loaded-inert, or loaded-determinative

focus: [D3-A14/D3] routing observability/telemetry: emit routeTelemetry with mode chosen, alias matched, bundle-loaded state, proof verdict, and separate telemetryMissingRate from routeMissRate

graphEvents.label: How can sk-design make parent-hub routing observable enough to measure missing telemetry and actual route misses separately?
sk-design route facts exist in hub prose and mode-registry rows, but no emitted route event carries workflowMode, matched alias, default/defer state, packet, or backend
Context-loaded and proof-of-application cards expose bundle/proof verdicts after routing, but they are not joined to the chosen route
The skill-benchmark observed-result shape, live executor, funnel, and report lack a hard routeTelemetry lane or miss-rate aggregate
Read-only local corpus measurement found 55 prompt-bearing sk-design playbook scenarios and zero machine-readable route telemetry records, telemetryMissingRate=1.000
Resource recall, context proof, proof-card readiness, and utilization credit may score only after a routeTelemetry event is present and valid when expected
Reports must separate telemetryMissingRate from routeMissRate so instrumentation absence is not collapsed into routing defect rate

focus: [D4-A1/D4] wired-MCP direct-tool bypass: a PreToolUse matcher on the open-design MCP mutating tools so the precondition checks at the tool boundary

graphEvents.label: How can Open Design mutating MCP calls be blocked unless sk-design context was loaded and carried into the tool input?
mcp-open-design states a hard sk-design precondition, but the checked docs implement it as skill/router prose rather than a concrete tool-boundary matcher
Open Design's mutating/destructive MCP tool set is concrete enough for a deny-by-default matcher: create_artifact, write_file, create_project, start_run, cancel_run, delete_file, delete_project
The registered Codex PreToolUse hook currently denies only Bash commands and explicitly returns no decision for non-Bash tools, leaving direct MCP tools unguarded
The local OpenCode plugin entrypoints are prompt/status surfaces today, so OpenCode parity needs a verified pre-tool interception surface or a guarded MCP proxy
Any Open Design mutating/destructive tool call that can affect a design output must carry a valid content-bound skDesignGate token before execution
A direct MCP start_run/create/write/cancel/delete call without skDesignGate must fail closed before the Open Design daemon receives it

focus: [D4-A2/D4] od CLI Bash bypass: PreToolUse matcher for daemon-cli.mjs write verbs run start/ui respond/media generate

graphEvents.label: How can Codex PreToolUse block Open Design CLI write verbs invoked through Bash unless sk-design context is carried into the command?
The installed daemon-cli.mjs and CLI chunk expose concrete write verbs run start, ui respond, and media generate that can be matched at the Bash boundary
The current Codex PreToolUse policy is Bash-only and literal-denylist based, so it needs a structured odCliPreconditions lane rather than raw patterns like run start
A CLI skDesignGate cannot live only in transcript prose; the Bash hook needs an inspectable same-command gate file or equivalent token pointer
A Bash matcher closes daemon-cli.mjs shell-outs but does not cover direct MCP, HTTP, in-app Skills, or child runtimes outside the hook envelope
Any recognized daemon-cli.mjs design-affecting write verb must deny before execution unless a current content-bound skDesignGate is present
The matcher must bind Open Design CLI identity and mutating verb in the same command segment; verb-only patterns are too broad

focus: [D4-A3/D4] the exemption is self-judged + post-hoc — define it on the tool, not the intent

graphEvents.label: How can pure transport be exempt without letting design-feeding Open Design reads self-attest after the fact?
The current pure-transport exemption is driven by caller/model intent such as feeds_design_decision, not by inspectable Open Design tool-call data
tool_surface.md says read-only Open Design tools are always safe to surface, while the mandatory design gate says read-only design-feeding calls must block without sk-design
sk-design proof cards prove context shape and READY fields, but not Open Design read-output lineage or whether a pure-transport read was later used as design grounding
Every ambiguous Open Design read must carry an inspectable openDesignUse or receipt before it can be used as design input
A pure-transport receipt may not be cited by a later design-affecting Open Design run or artifact decision

focus: [D4-A4/D4] the gate never executes: design_gate/require_sk_interface_design are pseudocode; minimum executable PreToolUse hook that returns deny

graphEvents.label: What is the smallest executable PreToolUse gate that denies Open Design tool calls missing sk-design proof?
mcp-open-design has mandatory sk-design pairing prose and design_gate pseudocode, but no executing tool-boundary gate
The registered Codex PreToolUse hook can return decision:deny, but currently returns no decision for every non-Bash tool
The repo policy schema contains only bash denylist keys, so it cannot express guarded Open Design tools or skDesignGate requirements
The current hook intentionally fails open on errors, but an Open Design deny-by-default precondition must deny when gate validation errors
Guarded Open Design MCP calls must deny in PreToolUse before the Open Design daemon receives them when skDesignGate is absent or invalid
A validator error for a recognized guarded Open Design tool must deny rather than return an empty decision
Direct hook exercise denied Bash git reset --hard but returned empty decisions for open-design.start_run and mcp-open-design.start_run

focus: [D4-A5/D4] feeds_design_decision is caller-supplied and defaults false; invert to deny-by-default with a checkable positive exemption token

graphEvents.label: How should pure transport be positively exempted without letting unclassified Open Design reads become design inputs?
route_open_design_resources defaults feeds_design_decision to false, so absent purpose classification becomes an exemption rather than unclassified/denied
tool_surface.md says read-only Open Design tools are safe without ceremony, but provides no positive receipt proving a read stayed pure transport
skDesignGate is a positive authorization for design-feeding use and should not also represent a pure-transport non-design exemption
Missing or malformed Open Design purpose classification must default to unclassified and may not feed design decisions
Pure transport must pass only with a checkable openDesignExemption token bound to tool, input digest, output digest, purpose, expiry, and no-design-use constraints

focus: [MON-B3/D6] designer-skills-main 9-plugin command structure as task recipes over specialist skills; port command recipe grammar and utilization checks to D2/D3

graphEvents.label: How should designer-skills-main command recipes be ported to sk-design without replacing the registry hub?
designer-skills-main routes first by domain plugin package and task command, not by a central mode registry
External commands are task recipes that choreograph multiple named specialist skills and end with an output contract
External command chaining is explicit follow-up guidance, not silent auto-execution
sk-design context proof fields plus skill-benchmark router replay can enforce command recipe utilization on fixtures
Every design task command must declare argument grammar, required mode bundle, required packets/assets, output contract, and next-command handoff metadata
Command chaining must be represented as explicit NEXT_OPTIONS/HANDOFF metadata unless the user requests a multi-stage workflow

focus: [MON-B3/D6] designer-skills-main command grammar lowered into commandRecipe schema for D2/D3 fixture enforcement

graphEvents.label: What fields must a sk-design command recipe carry so command routing and utilization are fixture-checkable?
designer-skills-main argument hints are typed task inputs such as product/feature, CSS/design file, screen/component, initiative, Figma URL, image, or design decision
External commands encode ordered specialist-skill choreography, which is the missing utilization unit for sk-design commands
External chaining is explicit follow-up suggestion grammar, not silent continuation, so sk-design needs parseable NEXT_OPTIONS/HANDOFF fields
The deep-improvement skill-benchmark D2/D3 router-replay lane can accept a commandRecipe adapter before resource recall
Every task command must declare input class, accepted forms, examples, rejection conditions, produced artifact, and route hint
Every task command with multi-mode work must declare ordered choreography steps with required mode/packet/assets and proof fields
Command chaining must be represented as nextOptions/handoff metadata and may not auto-execute without explicit user request

focus: [D4-A8/D4] inner-generator context loss: start_run --agent spawns a second model with no sk-design; compile judgment into brief and form answers

graphEvents.label: How can sk-design judgment survive the Open Design start_run inner-agent boundary?
start_run/od run start spawns an inner agent and only the prompt/message plus inputs are guaranteed payload carriers
The discovery-form answer fires the build and can bypass sk-design if it is raw skip/default text
skDesignGate needs briefDigest and formAnswersDigest to bind loaded context to Open Design payloads
The inner agent/model selection must be part of the proof because opencode without --model uses a default model
A design-authorized Open Design start_run must carry a compiledOpenDesignBrief derived from sk-design judgment
A design-authorized Open Design form response must carry compiledFormAnswers bound to skDesignGate
The skDesignGate brief/form digests must match the outgoing Open Design payload before the daemon receives it

focus: [D4-A9/D4] sub-agent/Task delegation laundering: proof token must travel in dispatch payload and re-validate at the child's Open Design tool boundary

graphEvents.label: How can sk-design proof survive sub-agent/Task delegation before a child calls Open Design?
Agent I/O evidence is optional/advisory and cannot be the mandatory design proof-token carrier by itself
cli-opencode has a design proof-card scaffold, but the cli family lacks a shared mandatory design proof-token payload
A child must re-validate the design proof token immediately before Open Design mutating tool calls
The parent must demand back token/result/tool-call digests and replay them before accepting delegated design output
Design/Open Design child dispatches must carry DESIGN_PROOF_TOKEN v1 in the payload
Child Open Design mutation calls must deny missing, expired, or payload-mismatched design proof tokens
Parents must accept delegated design/Open Design results only after token/result/tool-call replay succeeds

focus: [MON-B9/D6] designer-skills-main residual build/visual craft: observation-triad, decision-rationale, accessibility-coverage, and naming/documentation lint as conditional sk-design proof lanes

graphEvents.label: designer-skills-main adds a neutral observation/problem/fix audit shape that can become a checkable sk-design finding schema
designer-skills-main design-rationale supplies alternatives/tradeoffs/validation fields missing from the current four-field sk-design proof card
designer-skills-main accessibility testing splits readiness into automated/manual/assistive/user layers rather than one generic accessibility dimension
designer-skills-main design-system naming and documentation templates can become conditional lintable artifact contracts
sk-design READY/adoption claims should require conditional proof lanes keyed to the claim type and produced artifact

focus: [D4-A11/D4] temporal decay/freshness binding - proof token TTL + invalidate when brief/subject changes

graphEvents.label: How should design proof tokens expire and invalidate when the Open Design brief or subject changes?
mcp-open-design requires sk-design before design work, but the checked contract has no TTL or subject-change invalidation rule
sk-design context and proof cards gate loaded files and READY fields, but carry no issuedAt, expiresAt, subjectDigest, or payload freshness check
Prior skDesignGate and DESIGN_PROOF_TOKEN sketches include expiresAt, but do not define max age, single-use semantics, or invalidation when subject/brief fields change
Open Design already uses roughly five-minute active-context expiry, giving a local precedent for short-lived gate material
A design proof token is valid only while unexpired and while subjectDigest, briefDigest, formAnswersDigest, route/mode bundle, loaded-file hashes, and Open Design lineage match the current tool call

focus: [D4-A12/D4] headless automation time-bomb - od automation schedules/fires generation with no later agent turn; freeze proof token at schedule creation

graphEvents.label: Where should DESIGN_PROOF_TOKEN v1 be frozen for scheduled Open Design automation that may fire without an agent turn?
od automation is a delayed headless mutation surface, so a later scheduled fire may have no agent turn to load sk-design
Fire-time-only validation is too late for unattended scheduled design generation; the proof must be minted at schedule creation and replayed later
Current sk-design proof cards and proof_check.py cannot represent routine id, schedule spec, planned mutating tools, or future-fire replay
Recurring schedules need explicit max-run/max-age review cadence rather than unlimited token replay
Any Open Design automation schedule that can later perform design-feeding mutations must freeze DESIGN_PROOF_TOKEN v1.automationBinding at creation time
Scheduled automation fires may only replay a frozen proof binding and must deny absent, expired, consumed, routine-mismatched, schedule-mismatched, subject-mismatched, or payload-mismatched tokens
Recurring design-generation automation must carry singleFire or bounded reviewWindow semantics, never an immortal proof token

focus: [D4-A13/D4] HTTP/fourth-surface escape - only daemon-side Open Design run/build precondition covers CLI, MCP, HTTP, and Skills

graphEvents.label: Where must the Open Design design-proof precondition live so it covers CLI, MCP, HTTP, and in-app Skills?
Open Design documents four interchangeable daemon-backed surfaces, so MCP-only or Bash-only enforcement is incomplete
The current sk-design precondition is strong in skill docs, pseudocode, and manual playbooks, but not an executable shared daemon invariant
The design proof token must be normalized as structured daemon input or metadata before start_run/build, not hidden in prompt prose
A daemon gate must classify operation purpose so pure transport stays exempt while design grounding/generation/form answers require proof
Every Open Design surface that can start or fire design generation must pass through one daemon-side DESIGN_PROOF_TOKEN v1 validator before the inner agent or build begins
CLI, MCP, HTTP, Skills, and automation requests should normalize into one envelope carrying surface, operation, project/conversation, compiled payload digests, and designProofToken
Pure wiring and bare inventory are exempt by operation purpose, not by surface name; later design use requires tokenized grounding or re-read

focus: [MON-B3/D6] designer-skills-main 9-plugin command recipes: routing/utilization, argument grammar, and chaining patterns for D2/D3

graphEvents.label: How should designer-skills-main command structure be ported into sk-design D2/D3 enforcement?
designer-skills-main routes by plugin namespace plus task verb command rather than only a parent-mode wrapper
Command argument-hint fields act as a typed grammar surface with examples and branchable input kinds
Command bodies declare ordered skill choreography that can become utilization proof requirements
Follow-up suggestions encode a lightweight command graph without silent auto-chaining
The corpus headline claims 30 design-practice commands while README tables and on-disk command files show 29
Each sk-design task command should compile to a commandRecipe with ownerModes, argumentGrammar, choreography, outputContract, and nextOptions
D3 utilization passes only when every declared command choreography step has a content-bound witness in the transcript or proof card
Wrappers, command metadata, registry owner modes, generated docs, and command files must pass structural drift checks before replay scoring

focus: [D5-A1/D5] dispatch-contract asymmetry: cli-* have a code-standards ALWAYS rule but no design twin - add a Design Standards Loading ALWAYS rule

graphEvents.label: Do cli-* dispatch contracts require design standards loading with the same force as code standards loading?
All three cli-* skills contain a Code Standards Loading ALWAYS rule for code review or generation dispatch
The cli-* ALWAYS sections do not contain an equivalent Design Standards Loading rule
sk-design shared context_loading_contract already defines dispatch manifests and proof fields for delegated prompts
Agent I/O evidence is optional-advisory and cannot be the only design enforcement carrier
Design/UI/Open Design-adjacent CLI dispatches must load sk-design, resolve the mode, carry a TASK TYPE dispatch manifest, and demand proof-of-application fields

focus: [D5-A2/D5] model the design manifest on the Gate-3 spec-folder pass-through: manifest present plus sk-design loaded plus register set, or ASK before dispatch

graphEvents.label: Can design dispatch use the same present-or-ASK pass-through shape as Gate-3 spec-folder delegation?
All three cli-* skills already require spec-folder pass-through or ASK before non-interactive delegation
sk-design already has context manifest, register-first gate, loaded-file checklist, proof fields, and deterministic proof checks
cli-opencode has a design/UI scaffold, but it is template-local and still permits an unknown register at dispatch time
Agent I/O is optional-advisory, so it cannot be the mandatory design manifest carrier
Design/UI/Open Design-adjacent CLI dispatches require DESIGN_DISPATCH_MANIFEST v1 before launching the child
A pre-dispatch design manifest must carry Brand or Product; otherwise the caller asks before non-interactive dispatch
Agent I/O may carry a design-manifest digest but must not be the hard enforcement authority under its current optional-advisory contract

focus: [D5-A3/D5] the mcp-open-design pairing is parent-resident - must be re-asserted transport-side inside the CLI child

graphEvents.label: How should a CLI child re-assert the sk-design plus mcp-open-design pairing before Open Design design-feeding work?
sk-design and mcp-open-design declare a mandatory transport/taste pair, but the declaration is local to the active skill context
The cli-* ALWAYS sections carry spec-folder and code-standards pass-through rules but no Open Design child-resident transport pairing rule
The cli-opencode design template requires sk-design proof but has no mcp-open-design transport branch
Open Design start_run is a multi-turn generation boundary, so parent validation needs a child transport result, not only final prose
Agent I/O is optional-advisory and cannot be the hard carrier for Open Design transport proof
Any CLI child asked to perform Open Design design-feeding work must load both sk-design and mcp-open-design inside the child runtime and emit OPEN_DESIGN_TRANSPORT_ASSERTION v1
Any CLI child that uses Open Design must return OPEN_DESIGN_TRANSPORT_RESULT v1 matching the dispatched design manifest and transport assertion digests

focus: [MON-B9/D6] designer-skills-main residual craft converted into sk-design D1/D3 craft-stress proof fixtures

graphEvents.label: Which designer-skills-main residual craft patterns should become sk-design routing and utilization fixtures?
designer-skills-main exposes a finite-state matrix shape for interactive UI proof that sk-design does not currently hard-gate
Readable measure and information density need measured proof fields, not only principle mentions
Broad i18n is already known, but sk-design lacks a conditional locale-stress proof branch for global or RTL component work
Lane C skill-benchmark can enforce these craft deltas through public/private routing and utilization fixtures
Craft proof lanes should fire only when the prompt creates or evaluates the relevant surface: interactive state, content density, or locale stress
D1/D3 craft-stress fixtures must use domain prompts publicly and keep expected modes/resources/proof fields in private gold

focus: [D5-A5/D5] smart-router blindness - add a DESIGN intent to each cli-* router or an ALWAYS baseline so routine design prompts load the design contract

graphEvents.label: Do cli-* smart routers recognize routine design prompts strongly enough to load the design dispatch contract?
cli-opencode, cli-codex, and cli-claude-code have no DESIGN intent lane in their smart-router dictionaries
cli-* ALWAYS rules pass spec folders and code standards but do not carry a design standards loading rule
cli-opencode has a design/UI task template, but it is not a cross-CLI router guarantee
Every cli-* dispatch surface must either route design prompts to a DESIGN contract resource or apply an ALWAYS design manifest baseline
